twitter_thread: Claude Code trims 25 dependencies, leaving only 58, compared to similar AI frameworks.
I used Claude Code with Tradingview-MCP server to control 12 Tradingview Desktop instances simultaneously.
The wrktreez 0.1.0 release adds 5 new APIs to the Claude Code ecosystem, expanding its capabilities.
Neglecting Chrome DevTools Protocol config cost me 3 days of debugging with Claude Code.
Tradingview-MCP server and Claude Code integrate in under 10 lines of code, thanks to a new library.
To optimize Claude Code performance, I prioritize the top 3 dependencies responsible for 90% of latency.
What's the most creative way you've used Claude Code to automate a task? #claudecode #ai
pack.linkedin: Claude Code's 25 fewer dependencies result in a 30% reduction in integration time. For example, combining Claude Code with Tradingview-MCP server enables control of multiple Tradingview Desktop instances. The recent wrktreez 0.1.0 release introduces 5 new APIs, further expanding its ecosystem. However, I learned the hard way that neglecting to configure Chrome DevTools Protocol can lead to costly debugging. What approaches have you taken to streamline Claude Code development and optimize its performance?
